interactions.summary: Jiang argues population collapse and moral desensitization follow from borrowing against the future, then answers the Newton/2060 question with a new dark ages and an AI God fork; the host's ad interrupts before returning to whether today's God is AI.
interactions.speaker_attribution: Interview exchange: SPEAKER_00 is host Jack Neel or ad read; SPEAKER_01 is Jiang unless noted.

speaker_notes.note: Diarization is consistent enough for this packet: SPEAKER_00 carries host questions, intro, outro, or sponsor reads; SPEAKER_01 carries Jiang answers. Sponsor ad segments are not Jiang claims.

claims.claim: Jiang says modern middle-class abundance is an illusion financed by borrowing from the future, and war or supply-chain disruption will bring the bill due through famine and normalized brutality.
By 2060 Jiang predicts a new dark ages in which some elites try to create an AI God that makes people obedient, while others fight for freedom, imagination, and a new spiritual path.

signature_moments.moment: Modern abundance is a debt against the future whose bill comes due in war.
signature_moments.source_phrase: borrowed from the future and we've mortgaged the future
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It gives the collapse argument its moral economy.

signature_moments.moment: AI God is presented as a false end of history built on obedience.
signature_moments.source_phrase: once this AI God is in place
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It sharpens the anti-surveillance spiritual argument.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: A future artificial authority mistaken for God and used to make people obedient, rational, and controlled.
Jiang's term for the chaotic 2060 world of catastrophe, war, and no central authority.

chronology_notes.note: This packet repeats the 2060 forecast and ties it to Newton/world-end framing supplied by the host.

uncertainty_notes.note: Packet includes host sponsor/ad material; semantic claims should not treat ad copy as Jiang's argument.
